mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5 percent, labor market slack signals severe demand destruction and recessionary pressures, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal Sharpe ratio remains below zero across all post-2008 test periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer and builder confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive mean forward return, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of stable inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization and corporate output growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5 percent, or forward returns during rising INDPRO are negative in over 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the opportunity cost of holding equities relative to risk-free assets, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive FEDFUNDS changes yield positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of observations, or the rank correlation remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When real personal consumption expenditures growth exceeds 2 percent quarter-over-quarter, robust consumer spending supports corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns during high RSAFS growth periods are negative in more than 55 percent of cases, or the threshold model fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over 3-year out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms genuine labor market tightening and wage growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in over 60 percent of triggered instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 90 percent of its 2007 peak signals severe capacity contraction and supply-side distress, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of instances when INDPRO is below 90, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward tests.
